decisions.summary: Plan a complete docs-RAG feature: prebuilt SQLite index, `pan docs` retrieval commands, install-time index materialization, and prompt-time injection for Claude Code and Pi.

Use the existing `sync-sources` package layout for skill and hook corpus files; the docs corpus includes `docs/**/*.md`, `sync-sources/skills/*/SKILL.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `.claude/rules/*.md`, and optionally PRDs per docs config.
Keep model work at build/reindex time only: `pan docs query` and the prompt hook read the shipped SQLite index and must not call embedding models or remote APIs during user prompt submission.
Extend the existing Claude Code `user-prompt-submit-hook` instead of replacing it, preserving waiting-state events and current memory injection output before adding a `<panopticon-docs>` block.

Represent docs-RAG settings in `config.yaml` under `docs.*`, normalized through `src/lib/config-yaml.ts`, with global/project enablement, trigger regexes, budget caps, corpus toggles, and classifier disabled by default.

For auto-planning ambiguity around the Pi prompt hook API, require implementation to add the best available Pi extension prompt-submit interception and tests; if Pi exposes no such API, the work agent must stop and report the blocker rather than silently shipping Claude-only behavior.

hazards.summary: The current Claude Code UserPromptSubmit hook prints context only; printing the original prompt would likely duplicate the user prompt.
hazards.mitigation: Hook changes must preserve the current stdout contract: emit only supplemental context blocks, with existing memory context first and docs context second when triggered.

hazards.summary: Hook execution is on the hot path for every user prompt and must fail open quickly.
hazards.mitigation: Use short timeouts, no network/model calls in prompt-time docs retrieval, and return no docs context on errors while still emitting existing Panopticon events.

hazards.summary: FTS5 MATCH syntax rejects some arbitrary user prompts.
hazards.mitigation: Retriever must sanitize/tokenize user text into safe MATCH expressions and return no results, not throw, for malformed or empty queries.

hazards.summary: Embedding dependencies can bloat the package or introduce native/runtime install failures.
hazards.mitigation: Keep embedding generation isolated to build/reindex, make the shipped index self-contained for query-time use, verify package size/index size budgets, and avoid first-run model downloads.

hazards.summary: The existing setup hooks path mutates `~/.claude/settings.json` and already has safety guarantees against clobbering user settings.
hazards.mitigation: Reuse `readSettingsOrAbortSync`, backups, atomic writes, and delta hook registration; add docs hook registration without resetting or rewriting unrelated hook arrays.

hazards.summary: Pi extension prompt-submit capabilities are not established in the current codebase.
hazards.mitigation: Implementation must verify the Pi extension API and either wire a tested prompt injection path or stop with a clear blocker before marking the Pi acceptance criterion complete.

hazards.summary: Session-scoped disable and budget state need a reliable conversation/session key, but hook payloads can vary across harness versions.
hazards.mitigation: Parse `session_id`/equivalent from hook input when present, record fallback behavior explicitly, and cover missing-session payloads in tests.

sessionHistory.note: Auto-planning session started for PAN-1203 from existing orchestration context.

Read PAN-1203 PRD and inspected CLI registration, install/setup hooks, Claude UserPromptSubmit hook, Pi extension hooks, config normalization, package build scripts, and existing SQLite FTS/embedding patterns.
